name: Linked List
description: Implement non-contiguous node chains, pointer updates, and slow/fast runner checks.
learning_objectives: Navigate sequential node links using pointer assignments
Identify cycle loops using tortoise and hare runner pointer alignments

patterns.name: Fast and Slow Pointers
patterns.description: Use two pointers moving at different speeds to detect cycles or find list midpoints.

concepts.name: Linked List Cycle Detection
concepts.theory_markdown:
# Linked List Cycle Detection

Cycle detection is solved using Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm.

### The Core Idea

If a cycle exists, a fast pointer (moving 2 steps at a time) will eventually catch up and meet the slow pointer (moving 1 step at a time).
learning_objectives: Explain why the tortoise and hare are guaranteed to meet in a cycle
Trace pointer movements in loop nodes
